commit.message:
Fix owner filter to include pre-multi-tenant data

When grove-cloud sets X-Grove-Owner (Firebase UID), the vault
owner filter was querying owner_id = uid, which excluded all
existing data stored with owner_id = "" (before multi-tenant
support). Now matches both the specific owner AND empty
owner_id rows so legacy data remains visible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add post-start phantom purge and background patrol

The 3-round startup cleanup is insufficient because Supavisor can
reconnect phantom sessions AFTER our cleanup completes but BEFORE
our River client starts polling. The phantom then grabs jobs faster
than our real worker.

New approach:
1. After River client starts, immediately purge all other clients
2. Background goroutine purges phantom clients every 30 seconds
3. Each purge kills other PG sessions first, then removes River
   client/leader rows for any client that isn't us

This ensures phantoms are continuously suppressed, not just at startup.

commit.message:
Fix upload auth: use session access_token instead of anon key

The VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is in sb_publishable_* format — not a JWT.
supabase.functions.invoke() falls back to this key when the session JWT
isn't propagated, causing the gateway to reject with non-2xx.

Use supabase.auth.getSession() to get the actual session access_token
(always a proper JWT for authenticated admin users) and pass it directly
as the Bearer token. Keeps the raw fetch approach for full control.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Move Mermaid sankey comment after YAML frontmatter (#916)

The SankeyChartGenerator was emitting a `%% GENERATED FILE` comment
before the `---` YAML frontmatter block, but Mermaid requires `---`
to be the very first line for frontmatter parsing. This caused GitHub
to show "Parse error on line 1" instead of rendering the diagram.

Move the comment after the closing `---`. Add buildSrc JUnit test to
prevent regression and a frontmatter-first-line check in
validate-generated.sh. Regenerate README.md and CODE_ANALYSIS.md.

Closes bb-y1ec

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify BlockingAssignPartSelect → §11.5.1 [skip ci]

The test performs a blocking assignment to a constant part-select (bits [7:4]) of an 8-bit variable. §11.5.1 defines vector bit-select and part-select addressing, including non-indexed (constant) part-selects on the left-hand side of assignments. There is no more specific subclause.

fix: normaliza nomes de região para corrigir gráfico vazio

A API retorna regiões em MAIÚSCULAS ("NORTE", "CENTRO-OESTE") mas
REGIONS/REGION_COLORS usam title case ("Norte", "Centro-Oeste").
O dataKey das <Line> não batia com as chaves do chartData, então
Recharts não desenhava nenhuma linha.

Adiciona REGIAO_LABEL para normalizar antes de construir chartData.

fix: replace frontend demo sequences with proven examples

scored LOW even though they're dangerous. Replaced with sequences

LOW:    Lysozyme, GFP, Insulin — confirmed benign, correctly cleared

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make seed upsert challenges instead of insert-only

The seed used .onConflictDoNothing() which meant any challenge metadata
changes (matchType, timeLimitSecs, config, etc.) in the seed were silently
ignored after initial insert. This caused autoresearch to stay stuck with
matchType="single" and timeLimitSecs=2700 in production despite the seed
having the correct values.

Replace all challenge inserts with a seedChallenge() helper that does a
proper select-then-insert-or-update, ensuring every deploy syncs challenge
metadata to the desired state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix sed pattern to match backslash-escaped dollar in CMake configs

CMake exports \$<LINK_ONLY:...> (backslash-dollar) in target files.
The sed pattern needs \\$ to match both the backslash and dollar,
not just \$ (which matches only the dollar, leaving a stray backslash).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add user feedback feature (v1.6.0)

Adds a feedback form accessible from the hamburger menu for all users,
including unauthenticated guests on shared list views. Submissions are
stored directly in Supabase and surfaced in the admin dashboard with
category badges, email, message, and timestamp.

- New FeedbackModal with category select, message textarea, optional
  email pre-filled for signed-in users, and success/error states
- HamburgerMenu: "Send Feedback" button visible to all users (no auth
  check), wired to open the modal from App
- App: handleFeedback inserts to the feedback table (same direct
  Supabase pattern as organize_events), with user_id when signed in
- AdminDashboard: fetches feedback in parallel with get_admin_stats,
  renders a list below the stats grid with colored category badges
- index.css: styles for feedback modal fields and admin feedback list
- Bumps version 1.5.0 → 1.6.0

Note: requires the feedback table and RLS policies to be created in
Supabase before submissions will succeed (see plan for SQL).

- Update src/registry.ts with catalog merge methods (getMergedCatalog, agentExists, getAgentDefinition)

- Create integration tests: src/integration/__tests__/wshobson-catalog-integration.test.ts
- Add verification scripts: verify-integration.sh, verify-wshobson-integration.mjs

## GitHub Backend Integration
- Ultra-Ralph workflow with GitHub issues for audit trail
- GitHub projects for task tracking and visualization
- Full traceability: planning → execution → verification
- Test results logged to GitHub issue #6 (RALPH COMPLETE)

- ultra-ralph: Updated prerequisites, added Quick Start section

- ultra-review: Updated prerequisites
- ultra-debugging: Updated prerequisites
- ultra-pipeline: Updated prerequisites

4. Agent Definition - Sample agents have correct metadata

- src/wshobson-catalog.ts (NEW) - 109 agent definitions
- src/registry.ts - Catalog merge methods

commit.message:
Fix bob-watch startup: fill gap when queue is cold on boot

currentSongKey was null by default, matching null (no song) on first
poll so fillGap never fired. Use '__init__' sentinel so first poll
always triggers the state check regardless of queue state.

Add 14 diagnostic test points to 4-layer PCB, 0/0/0 DRC

body dimensions.
